item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Daaaaaaaaamn. That was really good! I loved the moment of "wait. Hold it." when SCP-4725 said "people could be dead in here" and i realised that this was in the end of death canon. Obvious +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I guess this succeeded in disturbing me since providing relief until a more permanent solution is found is rejected due to 'Our way only.' Keeping people from turning into a puddle of mush that flows into the sewer to merge with the lake of mush that exists forever more sounds something the EC could agree on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with DarkStuff in that this article basically requires you to read the whole canon before you can understand the interview log, but I honestly don't care if I'm gonna be completely honest. Anything coming out from EoD is a treat for me as it is one of my favorite canons by a long shot, and I don't necessarily think that every single SCP article that was made for a canon must absolutely stand on its own. While a regular ol' article or one with haphazard crosslinks must be able to stand on its own, canons have the responsibility of furthering the plot, and this SCP serves the function of making the readers start to dislike the Ethics Committee in EoD if they did not already. If this was the first EoD skip that I had ever read, then perhaps I wouldn't have immediately hopped into the canon like I did when I read "The Thing That Kills You", but I think that's okay if it serves a purpose within the canon, which this article certainly does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddamnit. I hate all the characters, good job making me feel things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Completely understandable. I’m sorry about that, but I hoped that even if it feels too rigidly written that you enjoyed reading ❤️❤️

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This does not work even slightly outside of the canon lore (and I’m not talking about the given that is End of Death, I’m saying characters and relations and what not) and even less so outside of your series within said canon. For me, SCPs must stand on their own, and this left me feeling lost. It most certainly was paced in such a way that it felt like it was assuming the reader already knew a bunch of stuff.

Stuff like “The Thing That Kills You”, “Halfterlife”, and “Poking Death w/ a Stick” work because they are paced and presented in such a way that they function as an article without you having to know the canon already. They could potentially be your introduction to the series. This does not do that. Thus I am downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Freudian slip I guess)

Thank you, though! I’m glad you like it!❤️❤️

item.title: Thoughts
item.content:encoded: I really like this article, as it shows other GOI alternative responses to Death Ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ayyyyy! Another AR article.

Overall a solid addition to End of Death. One minor nitpick though:

Dr. Mesmur: What, and you are? Is that what you think you're doing with all of those brains inside your head, Conwell?

I'm flattered but I think you accidentally Cornell's name here.

Either way, +1.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Another End of Death SCP? Who would have thought!

I did.

This skip was made due to Rimple's Draft Swap Round 2, and was originally SeraDomiCher's draft! I hope I did it justice!

This article also uses the Chaos Insurgency as DolphinSlugchugger plans to have in her future overhaul!

Thanks to KindlyTurtleRock, DolphinSlugchugger, Uncle Nicolini, 9Volt, Stallmantic, not_a_seagull, and MaliceAforethought for crit!
